title: leucanthemopsis pallida subsp. pseudopulverulenta
intro: Leucanthemopsis pallida subsp. pseudopulverulenta, a subspecies of the alpine daisy, is a flowering plant that belongs to the daisy family (Asteraceae). It is native to the mountainous regions of Europe and has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The plant has been shown to have several health benefits, including reducing fever, soothing digestive issues, and exhibiting antioxidant properties. In herbal preparations, Leucanthemopsis pallida subsp. pseudopulverulenta is often used as a tea or tincture, and is also used in traditional folk medicine to treat various ailments.
